chunks.text: John D. Kubiatowicz is a Professor in EECS at UC Berkeley. Contact information includes office at 673 Soda Hall, phone 510-643-6817, fax 642-5775, email kubitron@cs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are T/Th 1pm-2pm at 673 Soda. Links include Personal Homepage, Publications, Dissertations, Research Support, and contact for Damon Hinson at damonh@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is John Kubiatowicz's position and contact information?

chunks.text: Biography: He earned a double B.S. in Electrical Engineering and Physics in 1987, M.S. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science in 1993, and a PhD in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science with a minor in Physics in 1998, all from M.I.T. He joined UC Berkeley's EECS faculty in 1998. Current research involves designing wide-area storage utilities, developing secure protocols for privacy and denial-of-service resistance, and exploring Introspective Computing for adaptive systems.
chunks.question: What are John Kubiatowicz's educational background and research interests?
chunks.answer: MIT degrees in EE/CS; storage and security research.

chunks.text: Honors and awards include the Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching (2003), Scientific American 50 (2002), MoundsView High School Distinguished Alumni Award (2001), Berkeley IT Award for Excellence in Undergraduate CS Teaching (2000), Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers (PECASE, 2000), George M. Sprowls Award for best PhD thesis at MIT (1998), IBM Graduate Fellowship (1992-1994), and Best Paper at International Conference on Supercomputing (1993).
chunks.question: What notable awards has John Kubiatowicz received?

chunks.text: Education details: 1998 Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science with a minor in Physics from M.I.T., 1993 M.S. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from M.I.T., and 1987 B.S. in EE and Physics from M.I.T. Research areas are Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT) and Security (SEC), with focus on computer architecture, quantum computer design, internet-scale storage systems, and peer-to-peer networking. Research centers include Quantum Architecture Research.
chunks.question: What are John Kubiatowicz's degrees and research focuses?
chunks.answer: MIT degrees; OS, security, quantum computing.

chunks.text: Teaching schedule includes CS 162: Operating Systems and System Programming, held TuTh 15:30-16:59 at Hearst Field Annex A1. Selected publications span from 1993 to 2009, covering topics like fault-tolerant quantum architecture, manycore OS partitioning, quantum circuit speed, resilient overlay networks (Tapestry), OceanStore prototype (Pond), landmark routing (Brocade), erasure coding vs. replication, distributed object location, maintenance-free storage, wide-area data dissemination (Bayeux), and Alewife multiprocessor messaging.
chunks.question: What course does John Kubiatowicz teach and his publication topics?

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include IEEE ICRA Best Paper Award on Robotic Learning (2025), Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science (2003), DARPATech Significant Technical Achievement Award (2002), 50 Leading Young Scientists (2002), Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science (2001), NSF Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists & Engineers (PECASE, 2000), and Okawa Research Grant (1998).
chunks.question: What recent honors has John Kubiatowicz achieved?
